author: The Works of John Cassian
book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
Chapter XXI. The authority of Holy Scripture teaches that Christ existed from all eternity.

ccel.title: NPNF-211. Sulpitius Severus, Vincent of Lerins, John Cassian

source.title: NPNF-211. Sulpitius Severus, Vincent of Lerins, John Cassian

title: Chapter XXI. The authority of Holy Scripture teaches that Christ existed from all eternity.

source.title: NPNF-211. Sulpitius Severus, Vincent of Lerins, John Cassian

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: And yet to return to what we said before, though all these things are so, as we have stated: how do we read that Jesus Christ (whom you assert to be a mere man) was ever existing even before His birth of a Virgin, and how is He proclaimed by prophets and apostles as God even before the worlds?

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: As Paul says: “One Lord Jesus, through whom are all things.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: And elsewhere he says: “For in Christ were created all things in heaven and on earth, both visible and invisible.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: The Creed too, which is framed both by human and Divine authority, says: “I believe in God the Father, and in the Lord Jesus Christ, His Son.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: And after other clauses: “Very God of Very God; by whom both the worlds were framed and all things were made.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: And further: “Who for us came and was born of the Virgin Mary, and was crucified, and was buried.”
